info.title: Babelconnect Agent API
info.description:
Control-plane contract for **babelconnect-server** — the single API surface every
Babelconnect SDK (Go, TypeScript, Dart; later Python) targets.

This document is generated from the protobuf source of truth
(`babelconnect-proto/proto/babelconnect/v1/babelconnect.proto`) and describes the
**request/response plane**: the `Agent` gRPC service (native HTTP/2 + gRPC-web) and
the REST/JSON endpoints mapped from it. The server→client **event plane** — the
`StateUpdate` push stream delivered over `Session`/`Subscribe` — is documented
separately in the AsyncAPI spec (`docs/asyncapi/babelconnect.asyncapi.yaml`).

**Auth:** clients obtain an OAuth2 bearer token (password grant via `POST /oauth/token`,
or the `/auth/sso/*` SSO flow) and send it as `authorization: Bearer <token>` gRPC
metadata on every call — or as the `Authorization` header on the REST/JSON endpoints.

servers.description: Same origin as the babelconnect-server gRPC-web / REST / auth surface.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Same-origin OAuth2 / SSO endpoints proxied to the babelforce backend.

bearerAuth.description: OAuth2 bearer access token (obtained from `POST /oauth/token` or the SSO flow). Sent as `authorization: Bearer <token>` gRPC/gRPC-web metadata on every call, and as the `Authorization` header on the REST/JSON endpoints.

oauth2Password.description: Resource-owner password grant, proxied to the babelforce backend via `POST /oauth/token`. The `/auth/sso/*` endpoints are the SSO alternative.

current.title: current
current.description: the account this session is currently scoped to (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.Account.title: Account
babelconnect.v1.Account.description:
Account is one tenant the signed-in agent can operate in — the switchable set
 (agent-role only) surfaced for the account picker.

babelconnect.v1.Ack.title: Ack
babelconnect.v1.Ack.description:
Ack is the empty reply to Send. It only confirms the server accepted the
 command for processing — the resulting state (or an Error) is delivered on the
 Subscribe stream, never in this reply.

babelconnect.v1.AddConferenceMember.title: AddConferenceMember
babelconnect.v1.AddConferenceMember.description:
AddConferenceMember invites a participant — exactly one of agent_id / number.
 Starts a conference first if none is active. `hold_others` parks every other
 live, non-held member of the CURRENT conference before the invite goes out
 (default off) so they don't overhear the new invitee ringing; the server
 unholds them again once the invitee reaches a terminal state (added =
 joined, or failed/removed = rejected/timed out). No effect when starting a
 brand-new conference: StartConference(hold=true) already parks the original
 party unconditionally in that case.

presenceOptions.title: presence_options
presenceOptions.description:
Presence selector: the options the agent may switch to (AVAILABLE plus any
 configured pause reasons), and the current presence's exact name+label. The
 `presence` enum above stays the coarse bucket for icons/colors; these carry
 the precise presence name+label the client renders + selects. (proto babelconnect.v1.PresenceOption)

availableTags.title: available_tags
availableTags.description: recording tags the agent may apply (proto string)

alwaysRecordOutbound.title: always_record_outbound
alwaysRecordOutbound.description: account records every outbound automatically (proto bool)

username.title: username
username.description: Identity for the shell/Status surface (resolved at auth, no extra fetch). login / email (proto string)

accountName.title: account_name
accountName.description: account / company display name, when known (proto string)

lineBlocked.title: line_blocked
lineBlocked.description:
Involuntary line block: the ACD/platform marked the agent busy / unreachable /
 declined (a recoverable state cleared via ResetLineStatus) — distinct from a
 chosen "busy" presence the agent (or sign-out-as-busy) selected. Drives the
 line-blocked banner + Reset; a voluntary busy presence leaves this false. (proto bool)

accounts.title: accounts
accounts.description:
Accounts this agent may switch to without re-logging-in (agent-role only,
 resolved at auth). One is marked `current`. Drives the Account tab's
 switcher; empty/single ⇒ no picker. (proto babelconnect.v1.Account)

email.title: email
email.description:
The agent's own email — distinct from `username`, which is the *login*
 identity and may differ. Populated for the embedding bridge's legacy-shaped
 `agent.loaded` payload so host pages can match/route on it. (proto string)

smsCapableNumbers.title: sms_capable_numbers
smsCapableNumbers.description:
Subset of `available_numbers` the platform marks SMS-capable. Drives the
 SMS composer's From picker; empty/unknown ⇒ client falls back to
 `available_numbers`. (proto string)

campaignStatus.title: campaign_status
campaignStatus.description:
Outbound-dialer (predictive campaign) membership state, kept live by the
 server. Unset/UNSPECIFIED means the agent has never touched a campaign
 this session; explicit OFFLINE means they left/aren't in one. (proto babelconnect.v1.CampaignStatus)

babelconnect.v1.AgentInfo.title: AgentInfo
babelconnect.v1.AgentInfo.description: AgentInfo is the agent block of the view (identity + live presence + caps).

activeCalls.title: active_calls
activeCalls.description: calls currently in flight for this agent (proto babelconnect.v1.CallState)

sms.title: sms
sms.description: SMS thread summaries (full history on demand) (proto babelconnect.v1.SmsConversation)

config.title: config
config.description: which surfaces this deployment/account shows (proto babelconnect.v1.AppConfig)

babelconnect.v1.AgentView.title: AgentView
babelconnect.v1.AgentView.description:
AgentView is the complete per-agent state the server owns and renders to. A
 snapshot carries the whole view; patches carry entity-level deltas.

serverVersion.title: server_version
serverVersion.description: build version for the Status/About surface; reserve 8–14 (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.AppConfig.title: AppConfig
babelconnect.v1.AppConfig.description:
AppConfig is the server-resolved, per-agent feature configuration the client
 renders against (UI = f(view)): which surfaces are shown and their per-feature
 settings. Resolved server-side per deployment + account, so the client is a
 pure renderer. Carried on the snapshot (no patch) — config applies on next connect.

token.title: token
token.description: Optional: token may instead be supplied via "authorization" metadata. (proto string)

contact.title: contact
contact.description: phonebook label for the other party, if known (proto string)

time.title: time
time.description: unix seconds of the call (proto int64)

recordingUrl.title: recording_url
recordingUrl.description: playback URL when a recording exists (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.CallRecord.title: CallRecord
babelconnect.v1.CallRecord.description: CallRecord is one past call for the history list (a flattened conversation).

establishedAt.title: established_at
establishedAt.description: unix seconds when the call bridged (0 until then) (proto int64)

webrtcOffer.title: webrtc_offer
webrtcOffer.description: SDP offer the client answers; set while RINGING (proto string)

iceServers.title: ice_servers
iceServers.description: STUN/TURN servers the client applies; for off-host NAT traversal (proto babelconnect.v1.IceServer)

canTransferToApplication.title: can_transfer_to_application
canTransferToApplication.description:
can_transfer_to_application is server-computed: true for inbound calls and
 taken callbacks (source == CALL_SOURCE_CALLBACK), false otherwise. Plain
 outbound calls must not be offered application (IVR module) transfer
 targets — forwarding them into a queue module strands the agent
 (DEV-549/B2-526). Clients gate the "App" transfer-target segment on this
 flag instead of re-deriving the rule from direction/source themselves. (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.CallState.title: CallState
babelconnect.v1.CallState.description: CallState is one call as the server sees it. Replace-by-id on upsert.

babelconnect.v1.Campaign.title: Campaign
babelconnect.v1.Campaign.description:
Campaign is one outbound-dialer campaign the agent may join (the picker's
 entries). `test_mode` drives the "name (live)" / "name (test)" label.

babelconnect.v1.CampaignMemberState.title: CampaignMemberState
babelconnect.v1.CampaignMemberState.description:
CampaignMemberState is the predictive-dialer campaign membership
 lifecycle: offline -> logging_in -> waiting -> in_progress ->
 disposition (-> waiting for the next lead), with idle as the paused branch
 (waiting/logging_in <-> idle via pause/resume) and leave returning to
 offline from any state.

babelconnect.v1.CampaignMetrics.title: CampaignMetrics
babelconnect.v1.CampaignMetrics.description:
CampaignMetrics is the campaign's live pacing snapshot — always this exact
 6-key subset, never more.

since.title: since
since.description: unix seconds this state was entered/last confirmed (proto int64)

lead.title: lead
lead.description:
lead is the customer the dialer connected the agent to — set while a lead
 is being worked (IN_PROGRESS/DISPOSITION), unset otherwise. Updated the
 moment the dialer connects a lead. (proto babelconnect.v1.Lead)

metrics.title: metrics
metrics.description:
metrics is the campaign's live pacing snapshot (OBDD1), refreshed
 alongside the enrichment read while `state == IDLE` (paused) — the only
 state the Outbound tab shows it in; unset otherwise (and left unset if the
 GET /agent/outbound/metrics read 404s, e.g. between polls). (proto babelconnect.v1.CampaignMetrics)

babelconnect.v1.CampaignStatus.title: CampaignStatus
babelconnect.v1.CampaignStatus.description:
CampaignStatus is the agent's outbound-dialer campaign membership envelope,
 kept live by the server. `last_reason` is the platform's last dial-outcome
 as a lowercase string (e.g. "unreachable", "no_answer", "busy", "answer",
 "hungup", "none") — a string rather than a wire enum on purpose. `paused`
 is a convenience mirror of `state == CAMPAIGN_MEMBER_STATE_IDLE`. The lead
 the agent is currently dialing, disposition reasons, and live campaign
 metrics are additive fields below, layered on top of this envelope.

addConferenceMember.title: add_conference_member
addConferenceMember.description: invite an agent or number (proto babelconnect.v1.AddConferenceMember)

answer.title: answer
answer.description: SDP answer to a RINGING CallState (proto babelconnect.v1.AnswerCall)

disposeCall.title: dispose_call
disposeCall.description: record the call outcome, optionally with a callback (OBDC1/OBDC2) (proto babelconnect.v1.DisposeCall)

endCampaignCall.title: end_campaign_call
endCampaignCall.description: Outbound-dialer call/disposition controls (OBDB2/OBDC1/OBDC2 follow-up). end the live campaign call (OBDB2) (proto babelconnect.v1.EndCampaignCall)

endConference.title: end_conference
endConference.description: moderator ends the whole conference (proto babelconnect.v1.EndConference)

flagRecording.title: flag_recording
flagRecording.description: toggle the "flagged" tag on the recording (proto babelconnect.v1.FlagRecording)

joinCampaign.title: join_campaign
joinCampaign.description: Outbound-dialer campaign controls (OBDA1–3 CORE). enter a campaign (the picker's "Enter") (proto babelconnect.v1.JoinCampaign)

leaveCampaign.title: leave_campaign
leaveCampaign.description: leave the campaign (selector row or in-call) (proto babelconnect.v1.LeaveCampaign)

leaveConference.title: leave_conference
leaveConference.description: hang up only my own leg (proto babelconnect.v1.LeaveConference)

markConversationRead.title: mark_conversation_read
markConversationRead.description: clear unread on an SMS conversation (proto babelconnect.v1.MarkConversationRead)

pong.title: pong
pong.description: reply to a server Ping (liveness + RTT); echoes Ping.seq (proto babelconnect.v1.Pong)

register.title: register
register.description: announce reachability for this session (proto babelconnect.v1.Register)

sendSms.title: send_sms
sendSms.description: send an SMS (same as the SendSms RPC) (proto babelconnect.v1.SendSmsRequest)

setAgentNumber.title: set_agent_number
setAgentNumber.description: the external-telephone number to bridge to (proto babelconnect.v1.SetAgentNumber)

startConference.title: start_conference
startConference.description: open a conference around the current call (proto babelconnect.v1.StartConference)

wrapUpExtend.title: wrap_up_extend
wrapUpExtend.description: add time to the after-call-work countdown (proto babelconnect.v1.WrapUpExtend)

members.title: members
members.description: all members incl. the moderator and me (proto babelconnect.v1.ConferenceMember)

iAmModerator.title: i_am_moderator
iAmModerator.description: resolved server-side: is this agent the moderator? (proto bool)

myMemberId.title: my_member_id
myMemberId.description: the agent's own member id (used for Leave) (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.Conference.title: Conference
babelconnect.v1.Conference.description:
Conference is one multi-party call the agent is in. The server folds multi-party
 call updates into this entity and emits conference_upsert/remove patches. The
 client is a dumb renderer that shows the participant panel and sends conference
 intents. Usually 0 or 1 per agent. Upsert-by-id; removed when the conference
 finishes or the agent's own member leaves.

moderator.title: moderator
moderator.description: this member is the conference moderator (proto bool)

isMe.title: is_me
isMe.description: this member is the current agent (proto bool)

callId.title: call_id
callId.description: the member's call leg id (proto string)

agentId.title: agent_id
agentId.description: agent UUID when the member is an agent (proto string)

number.title: number
number.description: phone number when the member is an external party (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.ConferenceMember.title: ConferenceMember
babelconnect.v1.ConferenceMember.description:
ConferenceMember is one participant. `display` is the resolved label (agent
 name, else the caller/dialed number, else "Anonymous"). `state` drives the UI:
 pending = ringing, added = live, removing/removed/failed = gone (clients filter
 removed/failed out of the participant list).

babelconnect.v1.DisposeCall.title: DisposeCall
babelconnect.v1.DisposeCall.description:
DisposeCall records the outcome of a campaign call. `code` is one of
 ListDispositionsResponse's codes (e.g. "no_answer", "callback").
 `callback_date` is ISO `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mmZZ` and is set ONLY for the
 "callback" disposition — omitted (empty) otherwise.

babelconnect.v1.EndCampaignCall.title: EndCampaignCall
babelconnect.v1.EndCampaignCall.description:
EndCampaignCall ends the agent's live campaign call. Deliberately distinct
 from the generic Hangup: it also releases the dialer's channel and drives
 the campaign member-state transition to DISPOSITION, neither of which a
 plain media-plane hangup would trigger.

babelconnect.v1.EndConference.title: EndConference
babelconnect.v1.EndConference.description: EndConference tears down the whole conference (moderator only).

callId.title: call_id
callId.description: optional: the call the rejected command targeted (proto string)

allowStatusChange.title: allow_status_change
allowStatusChange.description:
Agents may change their own presence via the status picker. When false
 the picker is locked: still visible, shows the current status, but not
 interactive. Defaults to allowed. (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.FeatureAccount.title: FeatureAccount
babelconnect.v1.FeatureAccount.description: FeatureAccount gates the Account tab and its sections.

allowConference.title: allow_conference
allowConference.description: conference action (own bucket later when that surface lands) (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.FeatureCalls.title: FeatureCalls
babelconnect.v1.FeatureCalls.description:
FeatureCalls gates the call surface (Phone tab) and its in-call actions.
 Recording stays gated by AgentInfo.can_record (not duplicated here); device
 (browser/telephone) by AgentInfo.webrtc_enabled.

emitCallEvents.title: emit_call_events
emitCallEvents.description: emit cti.call to an embedding host on each transition (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.FeatureCti.title: FeatureCti
babelconnect.v1.FeatureCti.description: FeatureCti gates CTI/integration behavior (screen-pop + app→host call events).

babelconnect.v1.FlagRecording.title: FlagRecording
babelconnect.v1.FlagRecording.description: FlagRecording toggles the "flagged" mark on the call's active recording.

babelconnect.v1.GetStateRequest.title: GetStateRequest
babelconnect.v1.GetStateRequest.description:
GetStateRequest fetches the current AgentView (the unary "get current state").
 Empty; the bearer token rides in "authorization" metadata like every call.

babelconnect.v1.HistoryRequest.title: HistoryRequest
babelconnect.v1.HistoryRequest.description: HistoryRequest pages the agent's call history (newest first).

babelconnect.v1.HistoryResponse.title: HistoryResponse
babelconnect.v1.HistoryResponse.description: HistoryResponse is one page of past calls.

babelconnect.v1.HoldConferenceMember.title: HoldConferenceMember
babelconnect.v1.HoldConferenceMember.description: HoldConferenceMember holds or unholds an individual member (moderator only).

babelconnect.v1.IceServer.title: IceServer
babelconnect.v1.IceServer.description:
IceServer is a STUN/TURN server the client applies to its WebRTC peer
 connection (RTCIceServer shape). Carried on CallState so an off-host client
 (e.g. a phone) can relay media through TURN when it can't reach the server's
 host ICE candidates. Empty in the in-cluster/LAN case.

accountName.title: account_name
accountName.description: account / company display name, when known (ACC-E1) (proto string)

accounts.title: accounts
accounts.description: accounts this agent may switch to (ACC-E2) (proto babelconnect.v1.Account)

babelconnect.v1.JoinCampaign.title: JoinCampaign
babelconnect.v1.JoinCampaign.description:
JoinCampaign enters a campaign. The client normally sends `paused` false so
 the agent starts `waiting` for a lead right away.

babelconnect.v1.Keepalive.title: Keepalive
babelconnect.v1.Keepalive.description:
Keepalive is an empty heartbeat frame sent on the Subscribe stream so
 idle connections through proxies/LBs are not closed as idle. Notably the AWS
 Classic ELB in front of ingress-nginx (dev/prod EKS) has a 300s connection
 idle timeout; without these frames a quiet agent's gRPC-web server-stream is
 killed after 5 min. The server sends one every ~15s. It carries no state and
 does NOT advance `seq`; client caches treat a keepalive StateUpdate as a no-op.

babelconnect.v1.KickConferenceMember.title: KickConferenceMember
babelconnect.v1.KickConferenceMember.description: KickConferenceMember removes a member from the conference (moderator only).

id.title: id
id.description: internal lead-list-item id (uuid); may be empty (proto string)

uid.title: uid
uid.description: customer-provided lead identifier — the UI's "id" (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.Lead.title: Lead
babelconnect.v1.Lead.description:
Lead is the customer the outbound dialer connected the agent to. `id`/`rank`
 are only present on the richer (list-enriched) form some reads return. `id`
 is the internal lead-list-item id — DISTINCT from `uid`, the
 customer-provided identifier shown as the lead's id in the UI.

babelconnect.v1.LeaveCampaign.title: LeaveCampaign
babelconnect.v1.LeaveCampaign.description:
LeaveCampaign exits the current campaign (the server resolves which one).
 Available both from the campaign selector row and from within an active
 call.

babelconnect.v1.LeaveConference.title: LeaveConference
babelconnect.v1.LeaveConference.description: LeaveConference hangs up only the agent's own leg; the others stay connected.

babelconnect.v1.ListCampaignsRequest.title: ListCampaignsRequest
babelconnect.v1.ListCampaignsRequest.description:
ListCampaignsRequest / ListCampaignsResponse back the campaign picker
 (OBDA1). Empty request: the server always asks the backend for active
 campaigns only (`?active=true`) — there is no agent-facing use for
 inactive ones.

babelconnect.v1.ListDispositionsRequest.title: ListDispositionsRequest
babelconnect.v1.ListDispositionsRequest.description:
ListDispositionsRequest / ListDispositionsResponse back the disposition
 picker (OBDC1). Empty request: the list is a static global on the backend,
 not campaign- or agent-scoped.

babelconnect.v1.MarkConversationRead.title: MarkConversationRead
babelconnect.v1.MarkConversationRead.description:
MarkConversationRead clears the unread count on an SMS conversation (the agent
 opened its thread).

babelconnect.v1.MuteConferenceMember.title: MuteConferenceMember
babelconnect.v1.MuteConferenceMember.description: MuteConferenceMember mutes or unmutes an individual member (moderator only).

babelconnect.v1.Notification.title: Notification
babelconnect.v1.Notification.description:
Notification is a transient one-shot the client surfaces (a CTI screen-pop /
 pushed agent data). It is NOT stored in the AgentView snapshot — it fires once.

conferenceUpsert.title: conference_upsert
conferenceUpsert.description: upsert a conference by id (proto babelconnect.v1.Conference)

config.title: config
config.description: resolved feature config (emitted on register) (proto babelconnect.v1.AppConfig)

notification.title: notification
notification.description: transient CTI screen-pop (not part of the view) (proto babelconnect.v1.Notification)

smsRemove.title: sms_remove
smsRemove.description: SMS conversation id to drop (proto string)

smsUpsert.title: sms_upsert
smsUpsert.description: upsert an SMS thread summary by id (proto babelconnect.v1.SmsConversation)

babelconnect.v1.Patch.title: Patch
babelconnect.v1.Patch.description:
Patch is an entity-level delta applied mechanically by the client cache:
 replace the agent block, upsert/remove a call by id, or set wrap-up.

babelconnect.v1.PauseCampaign.title: PauseCampaign
babelconnect.v1.PauseCampaign.description:
PauseCampaign pauses (`on=true`, POST .../pause) or resumes (`on=false`,
 POST .../unpause) the campaign — no request body either way (OBDA2), same
 on/off shape as Mute/Hold.

label.title: label
label.description: contact name, or "recent" for a recently dialed number (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.PhonebookEntry.title: PhonebookEntry
babelconnect.v1.PhonebookEntry.description:
PhonebookEntry is one dialable contact (or a recent number) for the dialer's
 autocomplete.

babelconnect.v1.PhonebookRequest.title: PhonebookRequest
babelconnect.v1.PhonebookRequest.description:
PhonebookRequest / PhonebookResponse re-pull the agent's merged contacts +
 recent numbers on demand (refresh). `query` filters by label/number server-side.

babelconnect.v1.Ping.title: Ping
babelconnect.v1.Ping.description:
Ping is a liveness probe the server sends on the heartbeat tick (replacing the
 bare Keepalive frame). The client echoes `seq` back in a Pong command; the server
 measures round-trip time (RTT) from the reply and tracks per-session liveness.
 Like Keepalive it keeps the connection warm, carries no state, and does NOT
 advance `seq` — caches ignore it, and the reply is handled in the client's stream
 loop, not the state reducer. (APP-A5/CALL-M6.)

displayAsTo.title: display_as_to
displayAsTo.description: service number shown to the consumer (proto string)

displayAsFrom.title: display_as_from
displayAsFrom.description: number shown to the agent (proto string)

record.title: record
record.description: start recording the call from answer (recording.autostart) (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.Pong.title: Pong
babelconnect.v1.Pong.description:
Pong replies to a server Ping (StateUpdate.ping), echoing its `seq` so the server
 can match it to the outstanding ping and compute RTT. Sent automatically by the
 SDK from its stream-receive loop — not a user intent. (APP-A5/CALL-M6.)

babelconnect.v1.PresenceOption.title: PresenceOption
babelconnect.v1.PresenceOption.description:
PresenceOption is one selectable agent presence — "Available" or a configured
 pause reason. `available` is whether the agent can receive calls in it.

babelconnect.v1.ResetLineStatus.title: ResetLineStatus
babelconnect.v1.ResetLineStatus.description:
ResetLineStatus clears a blocked line state (busy/unreachable/declined) so the
 agent can receive calls again.

babelconnect.v1.SendSmsRequest.title: SendSmsRequest
babelconnect.v1.SendSmsRequest.description:
SendSmsRequest sends an SMS. `from` defaults to the agent's SMS-capable number
 when empty. `session` carries opaque
 CTI/embedding correlation keys (e.g. from session.set), like PlaceCall.

babelconnect.v1.SetAgentNumber.title: SetAgentNumber
babelconnect.v1.SetAgentNumber.description: SetAgentNumber sets the external phone number the agent's calls bridge to.

babelconnect.v1.SetConversationOpen.title: SetConversationOpen
babelconnect.v1.SetConversationOpen.description:
SetConversationOpen opens (reopens) or closes (resolves) an SMS conversation.
 The server flips SmsConversation.open optimistically and re-confirms.

name.title: name
name.description: presence to switch to, e.g. "available" or a pause reason (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.SetRecordingTags.title: SetRecordingTags
babelconnect.v1.SetRecordingTags.description: SetRecordingTags replaces the tags on the call's active recording.

babelconnect.v1.SetWebrtc.title: SetWebrtc
babelconnect.v1.SetWebrtc.description:
SetWebrtc enables or disables the browser phone (WebRTC). Off = bridge calls to
 the external-telephone number instead.

lastTs.title: last_ts
lastTs.description: unix seconds of the latest message (proto int64)

contactLabel.title: contact_label
contactLabel.description: phonebook label for the peer, if known (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.SmsConversation.title: SmsConversation

babelconnect.v1.SmsMessage.title: SmsMessage
babelconnect.v1.SmsMessage.description:
SmsMessage is one message inside a conversation thread, fetched on demand via
 GetSmsThread (the AgentView only carries the lightweight SmsConversation
 summary, not the full history). Oldest-first; the client bubbles it by
 direction. `state` is the message status (received/sent/delivered/failed/
 scheduled) for display only.

babelconnect.v1.SmsThreadRequest.title: SmsThreadRequest
babelconnect.v1.SmsThreadRequest.description: SmsThreadRequest / SmsThreadResponse page the messages of one SMS conversation.

babelconnect.v1.StartConference.title: StartConference
babelconnect.v1.StartConference.description:
StartConference opens a conference around the agent's current call. `hold` puts
 the existing party on hold while the first new member is dialed.

babelconnect.v1.StartRecording.title: StartRecording
babelconnect.v1.StartRecording.description: StartRecording begins recording the given call.

babelconnect.v1.StateUpdate.title: StateUpdate
babelconnect.v1.StateUpdate.description:
StateUpdate is the single server → client message on the Subscribe stream. On
 open the client receives a snapshot; thereafter partial patches. `seq` is
 monotonic across snapshot+patch — a gap means the client should resubscribe
 for a fresh snapshot. `error` is an out-of-band notice (command rejection /
 server warning) and does NOT advance `seq`. `keepalive` is an empty transport
 heartbeat (see Keepalive) that likewise does NOT advance `seq` and carries no
 state — client caches must ignore it.

babelconnect.v1.StopRecording.title: StopRecording
babelconnect.v1.StopRecording.description: StopRecording ends the call's active recording.

babelconnect.v1.SubscribeRequest.title: SubscribeRequest

applicationId.title: application_id
applicationId.description: transfer to a queue / voice application (proto string)

babelconnect.v1.Transfer.title: Transfer
babelconnect.v1.Transfer.description:
Transfer hands the current call to a target. Exactly one target is set:
 `to` (external number), `agent_id` (another agent), or `application_id` (a
 queue/voice application). `warm` selects attended (consult-then-drop) vs
 blind/cold (single action).

number.title: number
number.description: NUMBER kind, and AGENT kind when it has a PSTN number (proto string)

category.title: category
category.description: APPLICATION only: the IVR module name (proto string)

browser.title: browser
browser.description: AGENT only: browser-phone-reachable, no PSTN number (proto bool)

babelconnect.v1.TransferTarget.title: TransferTarget
babelconnect.v1.TransferTarget.description:
TransferTarget is one autocomplete result for a blind transfer / add-member
 pick. `id` is the backend id to pass back on Transfer/AddConferenceMember
 (an agent or application UUID; for a synthetic NUMBER result it is the raw
 query, not a stable id — clients should send `number`, not `id`, for that
 kind). `category` is the IVR module name (APPLICATION only, e.g.
 "simpleMenu"); `browser` marks an agent reachable only via browser phone
 (no PSTN number) — AGENT only.

babelconnect.v1.TransferTargetKind.title: TransferTargetKind
babelconnect.v1.TransferTargetKind.description:
TransferTargetKind discriminates a TransferTarget. The wire type only ever
 carries agent / number / application (a UI may group results differently,
 e.g. a "phonebook" section, but that is presentation only).

babelconnect.v1.TransferTargetsRequest.title: TransferTargetsRequest
babelconnect.v1.TransferTargetsRequest.description:
TransferTargetsRequest / TransferTargetsResponse back the transfer-target
 autocomplete (blind transfer + add-member dialogs). `query` filters
 server-side; empty returns the default result set (a synthetic NUMBER
 target only appears once `query` looks like a number).

babelconnect.v1.WrapUpCancel.title: WrapUpCancel
babelconnect.v1.WrapUpCancel.description: WrapUpCancel ends the agent's after-call-work early.

seconds.title: seconds
seconds.description: extra wrap-up seconds to add (e.g. 30) (proto int32)

babelconnect.v1.WrapUpExtend.title: WrapUpExtend
babelconnect.v1.WrapUpExtend.description: WrapUpExtend adds time to the agent's after-call-work countdown.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is no limit on the number of messages.

type.description: A URL that acts as a globally unique identifier for the type of the serialized message. For example: `type.googleapis.com/google.rpc.ErrorInfo`. This is used to determine the schema of the data in the `value` field and is the discriminator for the `debug` field.

value.description: The Protobuf message, serialized as bytes and base64-encoded. The specific message type is identified by the `type` field.

debug.title: Debug
debug.description: Deserialized error detail payload. The 'type' field indicates the schema. This field is for easier debugging and should not be relied upon for application logic.

connect.error_details.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message, with an additional debug field for ConnectRPC error details.

post.description: Exchanges agent credentials for a bearer access token. Proxied verbatim to the backend OAuth2 endpoint by babelconnect-server (`backendAuthProxy`); the server adds no logic.
Proxied to the backend logout endpoint; carries the bearer token.
Proxied to the backend token-revocation endpoint; revokes the agent's access token on sign-out. The bearer is also carried for backends that authenticate the revoke request.

200.description: Revoked (best-effort; RFC 7009 returns 200 even for an unknown token).
Accepted (best-effort; returns 200 even for unknown emails to avoid account enumeration).

post.summary: Begin an SSO login (returns the IdP redirect URL)

200.description: IdP authorization URL to redirect the user to.

post.summary: Complete an SSO login (exchange the IdP code for a bearer token)

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Authorization code returned by the IdP.

post.description:
Subscribe + Send are the control stream, split into its server→client and
 client→server halves — the one shape every client uses (RPC-A4 retired the
 earlier bidi `Session` RPC, which needed HTTP/2 end-to-end and couldn't
 ride gRPC-web or a plain HTTP/1.1 ingress). Subscribe is the server→client
 half (StateUpdate: one snapshot on open, then entity-level patches; UI =
 f(AgentView)). Send is the client→server half (one Command per unary
 call). Together they key to the agent's one state store. Command
 rejections arrive as an Error on the Subscribe stream, never on Send's
 reply.

Authenticate validates the caller's bearer token and returns the resolved
 agent identity (id, account, presence).

get.description:
GetHistory returns the agent's past calls (the History tab). A unary query —
 history is on-demand reference data, not part of the live AgentView snapshot.

ListCampaigns lists the outbound-dialer campaigns the agent may join (the
 Outbound tab's campaign picker) — on-demand reference data like
 GetPhonebook/GetTransferTargets.

ListDispositions lists the available call-outcome codes for the disposition
 picker — on-demand reference data like ListCampaigns. The list is a static
 global, not campaign-scoped, so the request carries no campaign id.

GetPhonebook returns the agent's dial-from contacts + recent numbers (the
 Contacts tab). A unary query like GetHistory: the live AgentView carries a
 phonebook snapshot frozen at register (AgentInfo.phonebook); this re-pulls the
 merged contacts+recents list on demand (refresh) so a long-running session can
 see new contacts. `query` filters by label/number.

post.description:
SendSms sends an SMS and returns the (upserted) conversation summary. Exposed
 over REST as POST /v1/agent/sms and as the Command.send_sms intent on the
 stream — both send the same message.

get.description:
GetSmsThread returns the messages of one SMS conversation (the chat thread).
 A unary query like GetHistory — the live AgentView carries only the
 SmsConversation summary; the full thread is fetched on demand. conversation_id
 is a query param (it can be a peer phone number when no conversation id is
 available), so this stays distinct from the POST /v1/agent/sms send.

GetState returns the agent's current AgentView as a single unary call — the
 REST/web "get current state" (the Subscribe stream is the realtime twin). When
 no live session backs the caller, the server builds the snapshot on demand
 (agent + presence + wrap-up); live call state requires a stream.

GetTransferTargets autocompletes blind-transfer / add-member targets as the
 agent types — agents, raw numbers, and IVR applications. `query` filters
 server-side (substring across name/email/number for agents, name for
 applications); the lookup has no call-context param, so eligibility by call
 direction is a separate, already-server-computed flag the client applies
 itself (CallState.can_transfer_to_application hides APPLICATION results).
